body:
### Description

Hello. After upgrading to 0.12.1 (Arch Linux official repos), most of my LSPs stopped working. The only exception is clangd, which happens to be the only LSP I've defined a custom `cmd` for. My config is [here](https://github.com/masroof-maindak/.dotfiles/blob/main/.config/nvim/lua/lsp-config.lua); please note that it has not been changed in over 3 weeks.

NOTE: **When I downgrade to 0.11, everything works perfectly.**

I have confirmed that I am on the latest version of nvim-lspconfip. A fresh re-install of my config/plugins has also not resolved anything.

Previously created discussion on neovim/neovim [here](https://github.com/neovim/neovim/discussions/38971#).

Here is what I see under `lsp.log`:

```
[START][2026-04-12 23:17:03] LSP logging initiated
[ERROR][2026-04-12 23:17:03] /usr/share/nvim/runtime/lua/vim/lsp/log.lua:151	'invalid "bashls" config: /usr/share/nvim/runtime/lua/vim/lsp.lua:476: cmd: expected expected function or table with executable command, got nil'
[ERROR][2026-04-12 23:17:03] /usr/share/nvim/runtime/lua/vim/lsp/log.lua:151	'invalid "gopls" config: /usr/share/nvim/runtime/lua/vim/lsp.lua:476: cmd: expected expected function or table with executable command, got nil'
[ERROR][2026-04-12 23:17:03] /usr/share/nvim/runtime/lua/vim/lsp/log.lua:151	'invalid "lua_ls" config: /usr/share/nvim/runtime/lua/vim/lsp.lua:476: cmd: expected expected function or table with executable command, got nil'
[ERROR][2026-04-12 23:17:03] /usr/share/nvim/runtime/lua/vim/lsp/log.lua:151	'invalid "neocmakelsp" config: /usr/share/nvim/runtime/lua/vim/lsp.lua:476: cmd: expected expected function or table with executable command, got nil'
[ERROR][2026-04-12 23:17:03] /usr/share/nvim/runtime/lua/vim/lsp/log.lua:151	'invalid "ruff" config: /usr/share/nvim/runtime/lua/vim/lsp.lua:476: cmd: expected expected function or table with executable command, got nil'
[ERROR][2026-04-12 23:17:03] /usr/share/nvim/runtime/lua/vim/lsp/log.lua:151	'invalid "rust_analyzer" config: /usr/share/nvim/runtime/lua/vim/lsp.lua:476: cmd: expected expected function or table with executable command, got nil'
[ERROR][2026-04-12 23:17:03] /usr/share/nvim/runtime/lua/vim/lsp/log.lua:151	'invalid "tinymist" config: /usr/share/nvim/runtime/lua/vim/lsp.lua:476: cmd: expected expected function or table with executable command, got nil'
[ERROR][2026-04-12 23:17:03] /usr/share/nvim/runtime/lua/vim/lsp/log.lua:151	'invalid "ty" config: /usr/share/nvim/runtime/lua/vim/lsp.lua:476: cmd: expected expected function or table with executable command, got nil'
[ERROR][2026-04-12 23:17:03] /usr/share/nvim/runtime/lua/vim/lsp/log.lua:151	'invalid "vtsls" config: /usr/share/nvim/runtime/lua/vim/lsp.lua:476: cmd: expected expected function or table with executable command, got nil'
[ERROR][2026-04-12 23:17:22] /usr/share/nvim/runtime/lua/vim/lsp/log.lua:151	'invalid "bashls" config: /usr/share/nvim/runtime/lua/vim/lsp.lua:476: cmd: expected expected function or table with executable command, got nil'
[ERROR][2026-04-12 23:17:22] /usr/share/nvim/runtime/lua/vim/lsp/log.lua:151	'invalid "gopls" config: /usr/share/nvim/runtime/lua/vim/lsp.lua:476: cmd: expected expected function or table with executable command, got nil'
[ERROR][2026-04-12 23:17:22] /usr/share/nvim/runtime/lua/vim/lsp/log.lua:151	'invalid "lua_ls" config: /usr/share/nvim/runtime/lua/vim/lsp.lua:476: cmd: expected expected function or table with executable command, got nil'
[ERROR][2026-04-12 23:17:22] /usr/share/nvim/runtime/lua/vim/lsp/log.lua:151	'invalid "neocmakelsp" config: /usr/share/nvim/runtime/lua/vim/lsp.lua:476: cmd: expected expected function or table with executable command, got nil'
[ERROR][2026-04-12 23:17:22] /usr/share/nvim/runtime/lua/vim/lsp/log.lua:151	'invalid "ruff" config: /usr/share/nvim/runtime/lua/vim/lsp.lua:476: cmd: expected expected function or table with executable command, got nil'
[ERROR][2026-04-12 23:17:22] /usr/share/nvim/runtime/lua/vim/lsp/log.lua:151	'invalid "rust_analyzer" config: /usr/share/nvim/runtime/lua/vim/lsp.lua:476: cmd: expected expected function or table with executable command, got nil'
[ERROR][2026-04-12 23:17:22] /usr/share/nvim/runtime/lua/vim/lsp/log.lua:151	'invalid "tinymist" config: /usr/share/nvim/runtime/lua/vim/lsp.lua:476: cmd: expected expected function or table with executable command, got nil'
[ERROR][2026-04-12 23:17:22] /usr/share/nvim/runtime/lua/vim/lsp/log.lua:151	'invalid "ty" config: /usr/share/nvim/runtime/lua/vim/lsp.lua:476: cmd: expected expected function or table with executable command, got nil'
[ERROR][2026-04-12 23:17:22] /usr/share/nvim/runtime/lua/vim/lsp/log.lua:151	'invalid "vtsls" config: /usr/share/nvim/runtime/lua/vim/lsp.lua:476: cmd: expected expected function or table with executable command, got nil'
```

Notably, clangd is absent from this list.

Another reason I suspect nvim-lspconfig to not be active is that when I remove the 'filetypes' key from my clangd config, it runs on every file, whereas if nvim-lspconfig were working, the default FTs defined there would be checked.

---

I tried manually inspecting stuff within command mode, but to no avail...

```
:lua= vim.lsp.config["rust_analyzer"]
```

Results in

```
{                                                                                                                
  name = "rust_analyzer",                                                                                                 
  on_attach = <function 1>                                                                                                
}
```

Which is corroborated by the health check:

### :checkhealth vim.lsp

```
vim.lsp:                                                                    ✅

- LSP log level : WARN
- Log path: /home/maindak/.local/state/nvim/lsp.log
- Log size: 78 KB

vim.lsp: Active Features ~

vim.lsp: Active Clients ~
- No active clients

vim.lsp: Enabled Configurations ~
- bashls:
  - on_attach: <function @/home/maindak/.config/nvim/lua/lsp-config.lua:14>

- clangd:
  - cmd: { "clangd", "--background-index", "--clang-tidy", "--header-insertion=iwyu", "--completion-style=detailed", "--function-arg-placeholders", "--fallback-style=llvm" }
  - filetypes: c, cpp, objc, objcpp, cuda
  - init_options: {
      clangdFileStatus = true,
      completeUnimported = true,
      usePlaceholders = true
    }
  - on_attach: <function @/home/maindak/.config/nvim/lua/lsp-config.lua:14>

- gopls:
  - on_attach: <function @/home/maindak/.config/nvim/lua/lsp-config.lua:14>

- lua_ls:
  - on_attach: <function @/home/maindak/.config/nvim/lua/lsp-config.lua:14>
  - settings: {
      Lua = {
        diagnostics = {
          globals = { "vim", "MiniFiles", "MiniStatusline", "MiniBufremove" }
        },
        telemetry = {
          enable = false
        }
      }
    }

- neocmakelsp:
  - on_attach: <function @/home/maindak/.config/nvim/lua/lsp-config.lua:14>

- ruff:
  - on_attach: <function @/home/maindak/.config/nvim/lua/lsp-config.lua:14>

- rust_analyzer:
  - on_attach: <function @/home/maindak/.config/nvim/lua/lsp-config.lua:14>

- tinymist:
  - on_attach: <function @/home/maindak/.config/nvim/lua/lsp-config.lua:14>

- ty:
  - on_attach: <function @/home/maindak/.config/nvim/lua/lsp-config.lua:14>

- vtsls:
  - on_attach: <function @/home/maindak/.config/nvim/lua/lsp-config.lua:14>


vim.lsp: File Watcher ~
- file watching "(workspace/didChangeWatchedFiles)" disabled on all clients

vim.lsp: Position Encodings ~
- No active clients
```

I'd be grateful for any insight on this problem.

comment_data.body:
> I suspect nvim-lspconfig to not be active ...
> 
> I tried manually inspecting stuff within command mode, but to no avail...
> 
> ```
> :lua= vim.lsp.config["rust_analyzer"]
> ```
> 
> Results in
> 
> ```
> {                                                                                                                
>   name = "rust_analyzer",                                                                                                 
>   on_attach = <function 1>                                                                                                
> }
> ```
> 
> Which is corroborated by the health check:

Yes, that indicates that nvim-lspconfig is not found on your 'runtimepath'. That's unrelated to nvim-lspconfig, and Nvim 0.12. It just means you have a plugin or config that is messing with 'runtimepath', or some other step during your "upgrade to Nvim 0.12 / downgrade to 0.11" is changing directories or something in a way I can only guess.

You can check `:scriptnames` to see if nvim-lspconfig is listed there. And check `:echo &runtimepath`

> [ERROR][2026-04-12 23:17:22] ... 'invalid "ruff" config: ... lsp.lua:476: cmd: expected expected function or table with executable command, got nil'

This happens because `cmd` literally isn't on the `vim.lsp.config['ruff']` config, as shown in your checkhealth result.
Thank you for the response. It was indeed in neither of those places. I suppose I'll wait for a while; perhaps a rogue plugin update broke something...
I fixed it by removing the lazyload on the plugin via the BufReadPre event. Not sure why this has only manifested now...

> fixed it by removing the lazyload on the plugin via the BufReadPre event

can you show a code example, so others can find it here. I assume you are talking about a "LazyVim" feature.

> > fixed it by removing the lazyload on the plugin via the BufReadPre event
> 
> can you show a code example, so others can find it here. I assume you are talking about a "LazyVim" feature.

Lazy.nvim, the plugin manager, not LazyVim, the full-fledged config.

Code change [here](https://github.com/masroof-maindak/.dotfiles/commit/aa358f9308c5668166c7dfe1e6e2535dbdcf4c14).

title: nvim-lspconfig configs aren't applied after update to 0.12 (solution: don't mark as "lazy")
